input.headline: ‘Too little, too late:' Former UK government slammed for its initial COVID-19 response

raw.headline: ‘Too little, too late:' Former UK government slammed for its initial COVID-19 response

winner: Why did the UK government bring a shovel to the coronavirus party? Because they thought they could bury their initial response later!

candidates.text: Why did the UK government bring a shovel to the coronavirus party? Because they thought they could dig their way out of a pandemic... too little, too late!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Puns on 'dig their way out' and ties directly to the headline with 'too little, too late,' showing specificity and surprise."}

candidates.text: Why don't governments ever play hide and seek with a virus? Because they always show up too little, too late!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Puns on the headline phrase with a clever twist, concise and surprising."}

candidates.text: Why don't governments ever go on first dates? Because they always show up too little, too late!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat related to the headline but lacks specificity and surprise. It's a mild pun that feels a bit forced and generic."}

candidates.text: Why did the UK government bring a shovel to the coronavirus party? Because they thought they could bury their initial response later!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat clever with the shovel metaphor, but the connection to 'bury their initial response later' feels a bit forced and less specific to the headline's context."}

candidates.text: It seems our government finally caught up with the virus last week... they're now in self-isolation.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat relevant and has a mild twist, but lacks surprise and specificity. The reference to 'self-isolation' is generic and could apply to many situations."}
